summary: DHH celebrates the early traction of Active Record, released just ten days prior, noting five revisions and close to two hundred downloads. He emphasizes that developer feedback matters more than raw numbers. The post highlights early community encouragement as fuel for continued development. It captures a moment of nascent momentum for what would become one of the most influential ORM frameworks in web development.
key_points: Active Record had five revisions in its first ten days, showing rapid early iteration
Developer feedback and community encouragement matter more than download numbers
DHH frames Active Record's goal as being the best ORM for rapid application development
money_quotes: It's much more interesting to hear what the developers using it thinks.
This certainly helps my motivation for making Active Record the darn best ORM package rapid application development out there.
key_insight: Early community encouragement and developer feedback were the real validation for Active Record, not download numbers.
